zh.lifeSummary: 當Hans Georg Weber於1623年3月9日出生在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧林特海姆時，他的父親Hans Weber23歲，母親Anna Sigel29歲。他和Barbara Pfatteicher至少有4個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1689年11月24日享年66歲，埋葬在德國巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hans Georg Weber于1623年3月9日出生在巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧林特海姆时，他的父亲Hans Weber23岁，母亲Anna Sigel29岁。他和Barbara Pfatteicher至少有4个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1689年11月24日享年66岁，埋葬在德国巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧。

nameMeanings.name: Weber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a weaver, Middle High German wëber, German Weber, an agent derivative of weben ‘to weave’. This form of the surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, and the Netherlands. In Hungary it is mostly spelled Wéber and Véber. In Russia, Slovenia, Czechia, Slovakia, and Croatia it is (also) established in the Slavicized form Veber , which is in Czechia also spelled Vebr. Compare Waber , Weaber , Weaver , Webber , and Webre .

History: As a name of ultimately Swiss German origin the surname Weber is also established among the American Mennonites. However, descendants of the early Mennonite settlers, who came to PA in the early 18th century, mostly bear the Americanized form of the name (see Weaver ).

variants.name: Wieber
meanings: German:
from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wīg ‘battle, war’ + berht ‘bright’, ‘famous’.
southwestern form of   Weber  .

variants.name: Webert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German: variant of   Weber  .

variants.name: Waber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Isadore.  German Hans, Kurt.
German: from a Silesian dialect form for   Weber   ‘weaver’.
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Weaver
meanings: English: occupational name, from an agent derivative of Middle English weven ‘to weave’ (Old English wefan).

English: habitational name from a place on the Weaver river in Cheshire, now called Weaver Hall but recorded simply as Weuere in the 13th and 14th centuries. The river name is from Old English wēfer(e) ‘winding stream’.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘weaver’, for example German Weber , Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) Tkacz or Tkach , Hungarian Takács (see Takacs ), and Slovenian Tkalec, Tekavec or Veber .

variants.name: Veber
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic), Slovenian, Croatian, Serbian, and Czech; Hungarian (Véber); Slovak (also Véber): occupational name for a weaver, of German origin (see Weber ), often applied as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic occupational names or surnames.

variants.name: Weeber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Dieter.
German: variant of   Weber   ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Wever
meanings: North German and Dutch: occupational name for a weaver, Middle Low German wever, Middle Dutch wever(e). Compare Weber .
English: variant of   Weaver  .

variants.name: Textor
meanings: German and Dutch: humanistic surname, a translation into Latin of Weber or Wever , from Latin textor ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Weaber
meanings: Americanized form of German   Weber   ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Webre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Cecile, Marcel.
French: altered form of German   Weber   ‘weaver’. This surname is rare in France. In the US, it is found mainly in LA.
History: This surname was brought to LA by Jean Baptiste Webre born 1725 or 1727 in Alsace, France, died 1787 in LA. His ancestors were from Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

meanings: Pet form of   Johannes .

nameMeanings.name: Georg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When Hans Georg Weber was born on 9 March 1623, in Rintheim, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, his father, Hans Weber, was 23 and his mother, Anna Sigel, was 29. He had at least 4 sons and 4 daughters with Barbara Pfatteicher. He died on 24 November 1689, at the age of 66, and was buried in Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hans Georg Weber est né le 9 mars 1623, à Rintheim, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, son père, Hans Weber, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Anna Sigel, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles avec Barbara Pfatteicher. Il est décédé le 24 novembre 1689, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hans Georg Weber am 9. März 1623 in Rintheim, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hans Weber 23 und seine Mutter Anna Sigel 29. Er hatte mit Barbara Pfatteicher mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 24. November 1689 im Alter von 66 Jahren und wurde in Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hans Georg Weber è nato il il 9 marzo 1623 nel Rintheim, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, il suo padre, Hans Weber, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Anna Sigel, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie con Barbara Pfatteicher. Morì il 24 novembre 1689, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Hans Georg Weberが1623年3月9日にバデンベズイルクサムト カルルスルヘリンスエイムで生まれた時，父Hans Weberは23歳，母Anna Sigelは29歳であった。彼はBarbara Pfatteicherとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1689年11月24日に66歳で死去し，ドイツバデンベズイルクサムト カルルスルヘにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hans Georg Weber(이)가 1623년 3월 9일 바든 베지르크샘트 칼스루헤 린테임에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hans Weber(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Sigel(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Barbara Pfatteicher(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1689년 11월 24일 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 바든 베지르크샘트 칼스루헤에 묻혔다.

pt.9 March 1623–24 November 1689: 9 de março de 1623–24 de novembro de 1689
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hans Georg Weber nasceu em 9 de março de 1623, em Rintheim, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, seu pai, Hans Weber, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Anna Sigel, tinha 29 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas com Barbara Pfatteicher. Ele faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1689, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hans Georg Weber родился 9 марта 1623 г. в местности ринтим, безирксэмт карлсрух, баден, его отцу по имени Hans Weber было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Anna Sigel, 29. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери от Barbara Pfatteicher. Он умер 24 ноября 1689 г. в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности безирксэмт карлсрух, баден, Германия.

es.9 March 1623–24 November 1689: 9 de marzo de 1623–24 de noviembre de 1689
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hans Georg Weber nació el 9 de marzo de 1623, en Rintheim, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, su papá, Hans Weber, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Anna Sigel, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas con Barbara Pfatteicher. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1689, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Alemania.
